title: Kevin Warsh, new Fed Chair, stirs tension in global markets
summary: Warsh's international debut and his hawkish comments strengthen the dollar and cause declines in European stock markets, as investors digest the end of the easy money era.

title: Bullish outlook for artificial intelligence according to Citigroup, BlackRock and the Japanese market
summary: Analysts maintain their optimism in the AI sector, highlighting growth potential and rotation into related stocks.

title: Global stock markets close their best quarter in years
summary: Wall Street, the Ibex 35 and the S&P 500 record historic gains in the second quarter of 2026, driven by economic strength and risk appetite.

title: European banking restructures amid new regulatory and market challenges
summary: Deutsche Bank exits retail banking in India, Barclays acquires its London headquarters, Ibercaja expands its board, Addiko provisions for Swiss franc loans, and Volksbank seeks help.

title: Wave of corporate mergers and acquisitions across multiple sectors
summary: Perpetual rejects an offer from EQT, Audax pressures Elmera, KKR enters pensions, Schroders sells its financial division, Vedanta cancels its IPO, and AA and KNDS seek to list.

title: High interest rates redefine mortgages, savings, and social security
summary: Euribor continues to fall, savings accounts and CDs offer up to 4.10% APY, while changes to social security are debated and mortgages adjust.

title: The energy transition advances with investments in renewables, battery recycling, and fossil fuels
summary: Acciona Energía celebrates five years on the stock market, DOWA recovers metals from used batteries, and the U.S. will surpass China in fossil fuel spending.

title: Eurozone and German inflation falls more than expected in June
summary: The year-on-year inflation rate in the eurozone stands at 2.8%, while German inflation also falls more than expected.

title: Nike and JD Sports fall on the stock market after weak results
summary: Shares of both companies plummet after their revenues missed expectations, especially in the Chinese market.

title: Trump declares annual income over $1 billion, with strong ties to cryptocurrencies
summary: Donald Trump's financial disclosure reveals income of at least $1.4 billion and significant exposure to digital assets.

title: Lime, Uber's scooter company, debuts on the stock market with a $167 million raise
summary: Lime's initial public offering in the U.S. raises $167 million, bolstering the micromobility sector.

title: Gold heads for its worst quarter in over a decade, while silver remains stable
summary: The precious metal trades near $4,000 but accumulates significant quarterly losses, and silver remains at levels from late 2025.

title: MiCA cryptocurrency regulation comes into effect in the EU with low adoption
summary: Only a minority of EU crypto groups have obtained the license, while Spain authorizes its latest company under the new framework.

title: The Magnificent Seven lose $2.3 trillion and Nvidia falls to key levels
summary: The technology sector suffers a sharp setback, with major stocks losing market cap and Nvidia reaching demand zones.

title: European and Asian stock markets trade mixed due to geopolitical tensions and AI support
summary: Stalled talks between the U.S. and Iran weigh on sentiment, while artificial intelligence stocks support indices.

title: Commodity prices show mixed trends: gasoline stable, wheat down, and energy alliances
summary: Gasoline remains flat in Japan, wheat falls due to abundant supplies, and Eni and Mercuria partner to trade energy.

title: Currencies react to monetary policy expectations: weak euro, strong dollar, and yen on hold
summary: The euro falls against the dollar ahead of the Fed speech, while 'Mr. Yen' keeps uncertainty over possible intervention.

title: Experts warn about the risks of artificial intelligence for democracy and markets
summary: A UN report warns about linguistic inequality and influence on democracy, and the BoE proposes 'kill switches' for algorithmic trading.

title: China's economy shows signs of slowdown: PMI moderates and yuan weakens
summary: China's manufacturing PMI moderates in June and the yuan falls against the dollar, reflecting a loss of momentum.

title: Baby boomers and Wall Street heirs shape wealth trends
summary: A FT analysis points to a $60 trillion transfer to 'nepo babies,' while Handelsblatt highlights the economic power of baby boomers.
